questions.question: K'gari (Fraser Island) lies off the coast of which Australian state?
What is double pneumonia?
Which is the only country ending with the letter "K"?
Olivia Rodrigo's upcoming album is titled You Seem Pretty [what?] for a Girl So in Love.
In September 2025, which fast-food chain had the most branches in Australia?
What is the maximum number of points that can be scored per wave in professional (WSL) surfing?
Which is wider: Australia or the moon?
The name of which hearty Italian soup translates as "that which is served" in English?
Where was Pilates developed: a Belgian health spa, British prisoner-of-war camp or German Air Force Academy?
Which fictional island in Gulliver's Travels is inhabited by people only six inches (15 centimetres) tall?
What kind of plants are elkhorn, maidenhair and bird's-nest?
How much does it cost to post a standard postcard within Australia?
True or false: Akubra hats are made from kangaroo pelts?
What part of the atom has a negative charge?
Which three films comprise director Baz Luhrmann's "Red Curtain Trilogy"?
questions.answer: Strictly Ballroom, Romeo + Juliet, Moulin Rouge!

questions.question: What is the capacity of a standard bottle of wine in millilitres?
Which composer's Symphony No. 94 is known as the "Surprise" Symphony?
Which of these New Zealand birds can fly: the kea, kākāpō or kiwi?
What word for a small fee to use a road is also a verb meaning to sound a bell?
Which crime novel was the most-borrowed book from Brisbane libraries in 2025?
The AFLW (Women's) season begins in which month?
The alleged last words of which Greek mathematician were, "Do not disturb my circles"?
In humans, what is the common name for the cuspids or eye teeth?
In which country are the cities of Byblos, Tripoli and Tyre?
The steepest angle at which a sloping surface of loose material remains stable without sliding is called the angle of… what?
